_comment: 草案:待你拍板。完全对齐 Thomson Ch.5-9 + Procaccia 的 8 个 content_root。

instances.name: Pareto 效率
instances.formal_form: No other allocation makes all agents weakly better off and at least one strictly better off.

instances.name: Kaldor-Hicks 改善
instances.formal_form: Winners could in principle compensate losers and still gain.

instances.name: Utilitarian 社会福利最大化
instances.formal_form: Maximize sum_i u_i(x_i) under feasibility.

value_roots.name: 上下界 / Ownership
value_roots.thomson_alignment: Ch.6.1 lower bounds and upper bounds

instances.name: Individual endowment lower bound
instances.formal_form: Each agent's assignment is at least as good as his endowment.

instances.name: Individual claim upper bound
instances.formal_form: No agent receives more than his claim (claims problems).

instances.name: No-envy (envy-freeness)
instances.formal_form: No agent strictly prefers another's assignment to his own.

instances.name: Symmetry / Anonymity
instances.formal_form: Two agents playing symmetric roles should be treated symmetrically.

instances.name: Rawlsian max-min
instances.formal_form: Maximize the welfare of the worst-off agent.

instances.name: Sen capability
instances.formal_form: Each agent's effective capability set should be expanded.

instances.name: Resource monotonicity
instances.formal_form: If social resources expand, every agent ends up at least as well off.

instances.name: Population monotonicity
instances.formal_form: When new agents arrive (without resource expansion), incumbents' welfare changes should be coherent.

value_roots.name: 视角鲁棒性
value_roots.thomson_alignment: Ch.8 robustness to choices of perspective

instances.name: Additivity
instances.formal_form: The rule applied to a union of problems equals the union of the rule applied separately.

instances.name: Consistency
instances.formal_form: If a rule gives x_i to each agent in two sub-problems, the same x_i is chosen for the union.

instances.name: Separability
instances.formal_form: Decisions about disjoint groups of agents do not affect each other.

instances.name: Dominant-strategy IC (Strategy-proofness)
instances.formal_form: Truth-telling is a dominant strategy.

instances.name: Bayesian IC
instances.formal_form: Truth-telling is optimal in expectation given the prior.

instances.name: Ex-post IC
instances.formal_form: Truth-telling is optimal for every realized type profile.

instances.name: Approximate ε-IC
instances.formal_form: Misreporting gains at most ε.

instances.name: Withholding-proofness
instances.formal_form: An agent cannot gain by withholding part of his endowment.

instances.name: Computational feasibility
instances.formal_form: The rule can be computed in polynomial time.

instances.name: Continuity
instances.formal_form: Small changes in problem data should not produce radical changes in outcome.

instances.name: Anonymity of agents
instances.formal_form: The rule's output does not depend on agents' identities.

instances.name: User-explainable
instances.formal_form: A user can understand why a particular outcome satisfies the axiom.

instances.name: Personalized explanation
instances.formal_form: A personalized justification can be generated for each user.
